sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.80 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: (K.\,W.\,Roggenkamp). a) Is it true that the number of$p 0.1ex$-blocks of defect~0 of a finite group$G$is equal to the number of the conjugacy classes of elements$g G$such that the number of solutions of the equation$[x,y]=g$in~$G$is not divisible by~$p$? The same question in the case of$G$being a simple group. { S.\,P.\,Strunkov} a) No it is not true; for example, if$p=2$and$G={ Z} _3 { S} _3$(L....

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
